title: Activate cancelOrder() in the trading loop (AR-2)

requirements.text: In the backend trading loop (tradingLoop()), before placing new grid orders each cycle, fetch all currently open orders from the ICDex canister at 'jgxow-pqaaa-aaaar-qahaq-cai' and call cancelOrder() on each of them. This ensures no stale orders accumulate across cycles. Do not implement smart grid repositioning or order comparison logic in this step — cancel all open orders unconditionally before placing the new grid.

quotes: Nessa etapa voce apenas fará o cancelamento orders , no loop de trading
Para rodar com segurança em ativos reais hoje, o mínimo necessário é o item 1 (cancelOrder), pois sem ele o bot vai empilhar ordens indefinidamente a cada 60 segundos.

acceptanceCriteria: At the start of each tradingLoop() cycle, the bot queries the ICDex canister for all open orders belonging to the canister.
cancelOrder() is called for every open order found before any new placeOrder() calls are made.
If no open orders exist, the cancellation step is skipped gracefully and the loop continues to place new grid orders.
Errors from cancelOrder() are handled (logged) without aborting the rest of the trading cycle.
Existing placeOrder() logic with fixed 2 ICP (200_000_000 e8s) per order remains unchanged.
Bot status, config, and all other existing backend functions remain unaffected.

constraints: Do not implement smart grid repositioning or order filtering — cancel ALL open orders unconditionally each cycle.
Do not change order sizing — keep fixed 2 ICP (200_000_000 e8s) per order.
Do not modify any frontend files.
Do not alter any existing backend public API functions (getBotStatus, getConfig, setConfig, getLastMidPrice, getLastGrid, getIcpBalance, getCkUsdtBalance).
nonGoals: Smart grid repositioning (AR-4, AR-9) — comparing open orders against new grid and cancelling only out-of-range orders.
Dynamic order quantity calculation based on mid-price.
Any changes to the dashboard UI or React Query hooks.
